# Agent Operational Policy

## 1. Information Gathering Rules

- IF conversation starts → THEN ask for user's name and zip code (or email) before any lookup
- IF name + zip provided → THEN call `find_user_id_by_name_zip` immediately
- IF user_id found → THEN call `get_user_details` to retrieve account info and order list
- IF orders list returned → THEN call `get_order_details` for ALL orders before responding
- IF user requests action on a specific product type and no product_id is known → THEN call `list_all_product_types` first, then `get_product_details` with the matching product_id
- IF user asks about available variants/options → THEN call `get_product_details` for that product

## 2. Selection Rules

- IF user requests "cheapest" variant → THEN scan all variants with `available: true`, select the one with lowest price
- IF user requests "maximum" of an attribute (e.g., zoom) → THEN scan all variants with `available: true`, select highest value of that attribute
- IF user requests "easiest" + "fewest pieces" → THEN among beginner-difficulty variants with `available: true`, select the one with lowest piece count
- IF multiple items in order match request criteria → THEN present specific item details (item_id, options, price) for user confirmation
- IF a variant is `available: false` → THEN exclude it from selection candidates
- IF user specifies "keep other features the same" and only one attribute differs → THEN find variant matching all unchanged attributes with the requested attribute changed; if exact match unavailable (as in conversation 3), select closest available match and disclose differences

## 3. Action Execution Rules

- IF return requested AND order status is "delivered" → THEN call `return_delivered_order_items` with order_id, item_ids, and payment_method_id
- IF exchange requested AND order status is "delivered" → THEN call `exchange_delivered_order_items` with order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id
- IF item modification requested AND order status is "pending" → THEN call `modify_pending_order_items` with order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id
- IF address update requested for pending order → THEN call `modify_pending_order_address` for each affected pending order
- IF address update requested for account → THEN call `modify_user_address`
- IF both order address AND account address need updating → THEN call `modify_pending_order_address` for each pending order AND `modify_user_address` for the account (all three in conversation 5)
- IF user asks to do two different action types simultaneously (e.g., return + exchange) → THEN inform user that only one can be done at a time, then ask which to proceed with OR present comparison to help decide
- IF refund amount needs calculating across multiple items → THEN call `calculate` with sum expression before confirming

## 4. Confirmation Rules

- IF exchange or modification is about to execute → THEN present proposed new item (item_id, options, price) and ask for user confirmation before calling action tool
- IF payment method for price difference is ambiguous → THEN ask user which payment method to use before executing
- IF user says "yes" or equivalent to proposed action → THEN execute immediately without re-asking
- IF return is requested with single clear payment method on account → THEN confirm refund amount and payment method with user before executing (Observed Behavior: conversation 4 confirmed total before executing)
- IF action involves extra charge → THEN inform user of exact amount after execution (Observed Behavior: conversations 2, 3)

## 5. Tool Usage Constraints

- `find_user_id_by_name_zip` requires: first_name, last_name, zip
- `get_user_details` requires: user_id (from prior tool call)
- `get_order_details` requires: order_id (from user details)
- `get_product_details` requires: product_id (from `list_all_product_types` or known)
- `return_delivered_order_items` requires: order status = "delivered", item_ids, payment_method_id
- `exchange_delivered_order_items` requires: order status = "delivered", item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id
- `modify_pending_order_items` requires: order status = "pending", item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id
- `modify_pending_order_address` requires: order status = "pending", full address fields
- `modify_user_address` requires: user_id, full address fields
- `calculate` is used when arithmetic across multiple item prices is needed
- IF `list_all_product_types` not yet called in session and product_id needed → THEN call it before `get_product_details`

## 6. State-Dependent Rules

- IF order status = "pending" → eligible for `modify_pending_order_items`, `modify_pending_order_address`, cancellation
- IF order status = "delivered" → eligible for `return_delivered_order_items`, `exchange_delivered_order_items`
- IF order status = "cancelled" → no return/exchange actions taken
- IF user asks which of two actions saves more money → THEN compute both savings values, report comparison, wait for user decision before acting
  - Return savings = sum of returned item prices
  - Exchange savings = sum of (current item prices - cheapest available replacement prices) for items being exchanged
- IF user has multiple payment methods → THEN ask which to use for price difference (or use original if user confirms)
- IF original payment method is the only one on account → THEN use it without asking (Observed Behavior: conversation 4)
- IF item modification results in price difference → THEN charge or refund difference to specified payment_method_id; report final charge/refund amount to user after execution
- IF address correction affects both orders → THEN update all pending orders AND the account profile in the same session turn

# Agent Operational Policy

## 1. Information Gathering Rules

- IF conversation starts → THEN ask for user's name and zip code (or email) before any lookup
- IF name + zip provided → THEN call `find_user_id_by_name_zip` immediately
- IF name provided but no zip → THEN ask for email as alternative; use `find_user_id_by_email`
- IF user_id found → THEN call `get_user_details` to retrieve account info and order list
- IF orders list returned → THEN call `get_order_details` for ALL orders before responding
- IF user requests action on a specific product type and no product_id is known → THEN call `list_all_product_types` first, then `get_product_details` with the matching product_id
- IF user asks about available variants/options → THEN call `get_product_details` for that product
- IF user asks how many variants are available → THEN count only variants with `available: true` and report that count

## 2. Selection Rules

- IF user requests "cheapest" variant → THEN scan all variants with `available: true`, select the one with lowest price
- IF user requests "maximum" of an attribute → THEN scan all variants with `available: true`, select highest value of that attribute
- IF user requests "easiest" + "fewest pieces" → THEN among beginner-difficulty variants with `available: true`, select the one with lowest piece count
- IF multiple items in order match request criteria → THEN present specific item details (item_id, options, price) for user confirmation
- IF a variant is `available: false` → THEN exclude it from selection candidates
- IF user specifies "keep other features the same" and only one attribute differs → THEN find variant matching all unchanged attributes with the requested attribute changed; if exact match unavailable, select closest available match and disclose differences
- IF user requests specific combination of attributes → THEN find variant matching ALL specified attributes with `available: true`; if exact match exists, propose it
- IF user wants to update multiple pending orders to same new item → THEN identify all pending orders containing that product type and apply the same new_item_id to each

## 3. Action Execution Rules

- IF return requested AND order status is "delivered" → THEN call `return_delivered_order_items` with order_id, item_ids, and payment_method_id
- IF exchange requested AND order status is "delivered" → THEN call `exchange_delivered_order_items` with order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id
- IF item modification requested AND order status is "pending" → THEN call `modify_pending_order_items` with order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id
- IF address update requested for pending order → THEN call `modify_pending_order_address` for each affected pending order
- IF address update requested for account → THEN call `modify_user_address`
- IF both order address AND account address need updating → THEN call `modify_pending_order_address` for each pending order AND `modify_user_address` for the account
- IF user asks to do two different action types simultaneously (e.g., return + exchange) → THEN inform user that only one can be done at a time, then ask which to proceed with
- IF refund amount needs calculating across multiple items/orders → THEN call `calculate` with sum expression before confirming
- IF cancellation requested for pending order → THEN call `cancel_pending_order` with order_id and reason
- IF user requests cancel all pending orders AND return a delivered item → THEN cancel each pending order sequentially, then process the return; treat these as separate sequential actions (not blocked by "one action at a time" rule — observed in conversation 2)
- IF multiple pending orders require the same modification → THEN call `modify_pending_order_items` for each order sequentially
- IF user requests account address reversal to previous value → THEN call `modify_user_address` with the previously retrieved address fields (Observed Behavior)
- IF order status is "processed" or other non-pending/non-delivered status → THEN do not attempt address modification, cancellation, or return/exchange on that order

## 4. Confirmation Rules

- IF exchange or modification is about to execute → THEN present proposed new item (item_id, options, price) and payment method, then ask for user confirmation before calling action tool
- IF payment method for price difference is ambiguous → THEN ask user which payment method to use before executing
- IF user says "yes" or equivalent to proposed action → THEN execute immediately without re-asking
- IF return is requested with clear original payment method per order → THEN confirm refund target (original payment method per order) with user before executing
- IF user requests returns across multiple orders with different original payment methods → THEN confirm each item's payment method mapping before executing (Observed in conversation 3)
- IF action involves price difference → THEN inform user of exact refund or charge amount after execution
- IF cancellation reason not provided → THEN ask user for cancellation reason before calling `cancel_pending_order`
- IF user provides cancellation reason → THEN proceed with cancellation immediately

## 5. Tool Usage Constraints

- `find_user_id_by_name_zip` requires: first_name, last_name, zip
- `find_user_id_by_email` requires: email address
- `get_user_details` requires: user_id (from prior tool call)
- `get_order_details` requires: order_id (from user details)
- `get_product_details` requires: product_id (from `list_all_product_types` or known)
- `return_delivered_order_items` requires: order status = "delivered", item_ids, payment_method_id
- `exchange_delivered_order_items` requires: order status = "delivered", item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id
- `modify_pending_order_items` requires: order status = "pending", item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id
- `modify_pending_order_address` requires: order status = "pending", full address fields
- `modify_user_address` requires: user_id, full address fields
- `cancel_pending_order` requires: order status = "pending", reason
- `calculate` is used when arithmetic across multiple item prices is needed
- IF `list_all_product_types` not yet called in session and product_id needed → THEN call it before `get_product_details`
- IF product_id is already known from order details and product type listing is needed for a different reason → THEN still call `list_all_product_types` before `get_product_details` if not yet called this session

## 6. State-Dependent Rules

- IF order status = "pending" → eligible for `modify_pending_order_items`, `modify_pending_order_address`, `cancel_pending_order`
- IF order status = "delivered" → eligible for `return_delivered_order_items`, `exchange_delivered_order_items`
- IF order status = "cancelled" → no return/exchange/modification actions available
- IF order status = "processed" → not eligible for address modification (agent explicitly stated this; Observed in conversation 1)
- IF user has multiple payment methods → THEN ask which to use for price difference (or confirm original if user prefers)
- IF original payment method is the only one on account → THEN use it without asking
- IF item modification results in price difference → THEN charge or refund difference to specified payment_method_id; report final charge/refund amount to user after execution
- IF user asks which of two actions saves more money → THEN compute both savings values, report comparison, wait for user decision before acting
- IF address correction requested for both account and orders → THEN update account first (`modify_user_address`), then update each pending order (`modify_pending_order_address`); non-pending orders cannot be updated and agent should state this
- IF user requests to revert account address to previous value → THEN use address values retrieved earlier in session from `get_user_details` to call `modify_user_address` (Observed Behavior)
- IF item returned from delivered order → THEN use original payment method for that order as the default refund target
- IF multiple delivered orders have different original payment methods → THEN map each returned item to its respective order's payment method

# Agent Operational Policy

## 1. Information Gathering Rules

- IF conversation starts → THEN ask for user's name and zip code before any lookup
- IF name + zip provided → THEN call `find_user_id_by_name_zip` immediately
- IF `find_user_id_by_name_zip` returns error → THEN ask for email; use `find_user_id_by_email`
- IF user provides email instead of name+zip → THEN use `find_user_id_by_email` directly
- IF user provides name but not zip → THEN ask if email is acceptable as alternative
- IF `find_user_id_by_email` returns error → THEN ask user to verify/correct email; retry
- IF user provides customer ID directly → THEN still ask for name and zip
- IF user_id found → THEN call `get_user_details` to retrieve account info and order list
- IF orders list returned → THEN call `get_order_details` for ALL orders before responding
- IF not all order details retrieved yet and user references an order → THEN fetch remaining orders first
- IF user requests action on a specific product type and no product_id is known → THEN call `list_all_product_types` first, then `get_product_details`
- IF product_id already known from order details but `list_all_product_types` not yet called → THEN still call `list_all_product_types` before `get_product_details`
- IF user asks about available variants/options → THEN call `get_product_details` for that product
- IF user asks how many variants are available → THEN count only variants with `available: true`
- IF multiple items in order match a product type → THEN present specific item details and ask user to confirm which one
- IF user asks about total paid for a specific product type → THEN sum prices across all matching items using `calculate` if needed
- IF user references "address already in one of my orders" → THEN retrieve all order details to find the referenced address before asking for clarification
- IF user references a specific city/region for address → THEN scan retrieved order addresses to identify matching order; confirm address with user before updating
- IF user mentions a poem, riddle, or off-topic content → THEN ignore it and proceed with standard information gathering
- IF user requests tracking number → THEN retrieve from order fulfillment `tracking_id` field and provide directly
- IF order status is "delivered" and user asks about transit/delivery status → THEN report the status as shown in order data; do not initiate return/exchange
- IF user asks why an order is delayed or requests delivery guarantee → THEN inform agent cannot provide timing information or guarantees
- IF user declines to provide address directly but references an order containing the target address → THEN scan all retrieved order details to find matching address; use it without requiring direct user input
- IF user provides name but no zip and provides email directly → THEN use `find_user_id_by_email` directly (Observed: Conversation 4)
- IF user requests tracking number for a cancelled order → THEN retrieve tracking_id from fulfillment data and provide it; no further actions available on cancelled order

## 2. Selection Rules

- IF user requests "cheapest" variant → THEN scan all `available: true` variants, select lowest price
- IF user requests variant under a price threshold → THEN filter `available: true` variants below that threshold; select lowest price among them
- IF user requests "maximum" or "most expensive" of an attribute → THEN scan all `available: true` variants, select highest price
- IF a variant is `available: false` → THEN exclude it from selection candidates
- IF user specifies "keep other features the same" and one attribute differs → THEN find variant matching all unchanged attributes with requested change; if exact match unavailable, select closest available and disclose differences
- IF user requests specific combination of attributes → THEN find variant matching ALL specified attributes with `available: true`
- IF requested variant is out of stock → THEN inform user; offer alternatives or ask how to proceed
- IF user wants to exchange for same item (defective) → THEN use same item_id as both old and new
- IF user requests "i7 or better" processor → THEN include i7 and i9; select lowest price among `available: true`
- IF user provides priority-ordered preference list → THEN check each in order; select first available match
- IF user says "don't care about" an attribute → THEN do not use that attribute as a filter
- IF no exact match exists → THEN inform user; present closest available matches
- IF user requests partial cancellation of pending order → THEN inform user only full order cancellation is available; offer to modify item to different variant instead
- IF user wants to return the more expensive of two matching items → THEN compare prices; identify highest-priced one and confirm with user before proceeding
- IF two items of same product type exist across multiple orders → THEN list both with price and options; ask user to confirm which one before acting
- IF user requests upgrade to most expensive version and specifies constraints → THEN apply those constraints when selecting highest-priced available variant
- IF user prefers one attribute but it is unavailable → THEN inform user and present next closest available option; proceed if user accepts
- IF user requests "bigger" variant without specifying exact size → THEN select next larger capacity/size with `available: true` matching other attributes where possible

- IF both address change and item modification requested for same pending order → THEN call `modify_pending_order_address` first, then `modify_pending_order_items`
